title: error pulling image "$FQIN": unable to pull $FQIN: unable to find image in the registries defined in "/etc/containers/registries.conf"
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When pulling or run w/ implied pull, an expired/invalid ``auth`` token, causes podman error with less than helpful message.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  ``podman login -u $USER -p $PASS my.registry.example.com``

2. ``$EDITOR ~/.docker/config.json`` - mess up the token for ``my.registry.example.com``

3.  ``podman pull my.registry.example.com/some_image:latest``

**Describe the results you received:**

Pulling image fails, then error displayed.  Tried adding registry to ``/etc/containers/registries.conf`` but that's not the source of the problem.  The only thing that fixes this is to ``podman logout my.registry.example.com``

**Describe the results you expected:**

``error pulling image "$FQIN": unable to authenticate with "my.registry.example.com"``

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Using Fedora 27

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.6.5
Go Version:    go1.9.7
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  MemFree: 840192000
  MemTotal: 12439457792
  SwapFree: 1073737728
  SwapTotal: 1073737728
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 4
  hostname: hostname.example.com
  kernel: 4.17.3-100.fc27.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 211h 35m 9.7s (Approximately 8.79 days)
insecure registries:
  registries:
  - my.registry.example.com
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - my.registry.example.com
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.override_kernel_check=true
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 14
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical machine

comments.body:
Update: It seems I can't consistently reproduce this via editing ``.docker/config.json`` (as user or root) some trickery is involved here.  Running ``sudo podman help login`` shows ``--authfile`` defaults to ``${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json``, however ``sudo cat ${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR}/containers/auth.json`` gives ``ENOENT``.  Even as root, the file doesn't exist.

The only way I can consistently reproduce this is by setting the registry to require auth for pull,  ``sudo podman logout``, then attempt pulling.  I have no idea why / how I reproduced this previously with ``.docker/config.json``
